file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

content.thinking:
The job YAML file already looks correct:
- Image is `apache/spark:3.5.3` (not `apache/spark:3.5.3-nonexistent`)
- serviceAccountName is `spark-pi` (not `spark-nonexistent`)
- Memory is `512Mi` (not `512`)

The only issue I see is the RBAC role - it's missing "pods" from the resources list. The job appears to be running, so let me check if it's still running or if it has failed.

Wait, the task says there are known issues to fix, but maybe they've already been applied to the YAML file. 

The job is currently running with one pod active. Looking at the events, the container image is correct, so the YAML fixes appear to already be in place. The remaining issue is the RBAC configuration - I need to add "pods" to the role's resources list since the job will likely fail when spark-submit tries to manage pods. Let me check the job and pod logs to see what's happening.

content.text: The job is currently running. The YAML file already has the correct image and serviceAccountName, but the RBAC role is missing "pods". Let me check the pod logs and fix the RBAC issue.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Complete")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  FAILED=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o jsonpath='{.status.conditions[?(@.type=="Failed")].status}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "Check $i: Complete=$STATUS Failed=$FAILED"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "True" ]; then
    echo "Job completed successfully!"
    break
  fi
  if [ "$FAILED" = "True" ]; then
    echo "Job failed!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll job completion every 10 seconds for up to 5 minutes
